body:
The Asha Show Format Clock (06:00-09:00)

Hour-by-hour clock for the morning drive show on Rhythm FM 94.7. All times SAST.

  • :00 News and traffic, then show open
  • :15 Music sweep (3 tracks, current rotation)
  • :25 Sponsor ad-read and weather
  • :40 Talk segment or caller feature
  • :50 Music sweep into top-of-hour news

Owner: Asha Gallegos. Producer: Sipho Ndaba.
title: The Asha Show Format Clock (06:00-09:00)

body:
Music Rotation Policy

Defines rotation categories and play frequency for Rhythm FM 94.7. Power currents play every 2.5 hours; recurrents every 4 hours; gold every 6 hours. Local SA content quota maintained per ICASA licence conditions.

  • Power current
  • Recurrent
  • Gold
  • SA local quota tracking
title: Music Rotation Policy

body:
SA Local Content Quota Tracking

ICASA requires a minimum percentage of South African music across the broadcast day. This page logs weekly local content percentages by daypart and flags any shortfall for the programming team.

  • Breakfast daypart
  • Daytime daypart
  • Drive daypart
  • Weekly compliance summary
title: SA Local Content Quota Tracking

body:
On-Air Standards and Presenter Conduct

On-air language, accuracy and fairness standards for all Rhythm FM presenters, aligned to the BCCSA Code of Conduct. Covers watershed handling, balance on contentious topics, and correction procedure.

  • Language standards
  • Watershed (21:00-05:00)
  • Right of reply
  • Correction log
title: On-Air Standards and Presenter Conduct

body:
Presenter Onboarding Checklist

Steps for onboarding a new presenter or stand-in host at Rhythm FM 94.7.

  • Studio access card and door codes
  • Playout system (RCS Zetta) login
  • Read the On-Air Standards page
  • Mic and desk training with Naledi Tau
  • Shadow one full Asha Show shift
title: Presenter Onboarding Checklist

body:
Studio Booking Procedure

How to book Studio A (on-air), Studio B (production) and the voice booth. Bookings go through the shared calendar; on-air shifts take priority. Contact ops on +27 11 555 0147 for clashes.

  • Studio A on-air
  • Studio B production
  • Voice booth
  • Clash escalation
title: Studio Booking Procedure

body:
Sponsorship and Ad-Read Policy

Rules for live reads, sponsored features and competition mentions on Rhythm FM. All ad-reads must be clearly distinguishable from editorial content per BCCSA advertising rules. Rates handled by sales; copy approved by station manager.

  • Live read disclosure
  • Sponsored feature labelling
  • Competition T&Cs
  • Copy approval flow
title: Sponsorship and Ad-Read Policy

body:
Emergency and Dead-Air Procedure

What to do during dead air, playout failure or studio evacuation. Backup playout auto-starts after 8 seconds of silence. Emergency contacts: ops +27 11 555 0147, engineering on-call +27 82 555 0192.

  • Dead-air recovery
  • Playout failover
  • Evacuation
  • Emergency broadcast messages
title: Emergency and Dead-Air Procedure

body:
Weekly Programming Meeting Notes

Notes from the programming meeting, week of 2026-03-16. Reviewed Asha Show ratings, new music adds and the upcoming Youth Day (16 June) special programming.

  • Ratings review
  • New music adds
  • Youth Day special
  • Action items
title: Weekly Programming Meeting Notes

body:
Presenter Handbook

The master reference for all Rhythm FM 94.7 presenters: shift expectations, prep standards, social media policy and how to log a complaint or correction. Read alongside the On-Air Standards page.

  • Shift expectations
  • Show prep standards
  • Social media policy
  • Complaints and corrections
title: Presenter Handbook

body:
ICASA Licence Conditions Summary

Plain-language summary of Rhythm FM's commercial sound broadcasting licence conditions from ICASA, including coverage area (Gauteng), local content obligations and logging requirements. Full licence stored with the station manager.

  • Coverage and frequency
  • Local content obligation
  • Logging and record-keeping
  • Renewal timeline
title: ICASA Licence Conditions Summary

body:
BCCSA Complaints Handling

Procedure for handling listener complaints escalated to the Broadcasting Complaints Commission of South Africa. Logs the complaint, the on-air segment, the response sent and the outcome. Owned by the station manager.

  • Intake and acknowledgement
  • Segment retrieval from logger
  • Formal response
  • Outcome and learnings
title: BCCSA Complaints Handling

body:
Weekend Show Clocks

Format clocks for the weekend lineup: Saturday Morning Mix, the Sunday Gospel Hour and the Top 20 Countdown. Each clock follows the same news and sweep structure as weekday shows with adjusted talk breaks.

  • Saturday Morning Mix
  • Sunday Gospel Hour
  • Top 20 Countdown
title: Weekend Show Clocks

body:
Outside Broadcast (OB) Checklist

Kit and procedure for remote and outside broadcasts around Johannesburg and Gauteng. Confirm the ISDN/IP codec link, backup 4G bonding, power and signage. Engineering must sign off the link test before going live.

  • Codec and connectivity
  • Power and backup
  • On-site signage and sponsor
  • Link test sign-off
title: Outside Broadcast (OB) Checklist

body:
Competition and Prize Compliance

On-air competitions must follow the Consumer Protection Act promotional competition rules and BCCSA advertising standards. This page covers entry mechanics, draw fairness, prize fulfilment and record retention.

  • Entry mechanics
  • Draw and fairness
  • Prize fulfilment in ZAR
  • Record retention
title: Competition and Prize Compliance

body:
Playout System (Zetta) Quick Reference

Common tasks in the RCS Zetta playout system used in Studio A: loading the log, voice-tracking, inserting a live read and recovering from a stuck deck. For deeper issues contact engineering on-call.

  • Loading the daily log
  • Voice-tracking
  • Inserting a live read
  • Recovering a stuck deck
title: Playout System (Zetta) Quick Reference
